experiment: GATE 1 — benign API-client confounder (token->API correlation FPR)
finding: The serviceaccount-token-exfil rule false-fires on 100% (25/25) of legitimate API-client correlation sessions. The token-read->kube-API correlation does NOT by itself distinguish a credential-exfil attacker from any benign client-go controller/operator/CronJob/kubectl-exec: identical syscalls, identical alert detail ('token-read container -> kube-api 10.43.0.1:443'). The headline FPR 0% held only because Online Boutique never contacts the kube-API.
implication: C3 'precision wall, not misconfiguration' is empirically FALSE as stated. The correlation is a high-RECALL signal but NOT precise without identity-scoping (service-account allowlist) or behavioral baselining.
